rawText:
The race to dominate artificial intelligence has become so expensive that the next major battle may be fought in public markets as much as in laboratories. OpenAI, Anthropic, and the xAI-SpaceX combination all need staggering amounts of capital to build data centers, train models, and serve growing demand. That financing pressure has turned three bitter rivals into potential mega-IPO candidates at almost the same moment.

The contest is not only financial, because each company is also selling a different story about what leadership in AI should look like. OpenAI presents itself as the broad commercial platform, Anthropic leans on safety and enterprise discipline, and Elon Musk frames xAI as part of a far larger mission. Their bosses also bring personal rivalry into every negotiation, which makes contracts, public statements, and fundraising rounds feel like moves in a long political game. When companies need cash, status, and strategic advantage at the same time, even ordinary business decisions start to look theatrical.

Those tensions are sharpest where business opportunity overlaps with government power. Recent fights over defense work and regulatory positioning show that technical competition is now entangled with lobbying, reputation, and access to the state. At the same time, none of the firms is close to comfortable self-financing, because revenue is rising quickly while infrastructure spending is rising even faster. That imbalance helps explain why public listing looks attractive even at enormous valuations and uncertain profit timelines.

If several giant offerings arrive together, markets may have to absorb a fundraising wave that would normally be spread across many years. Investors are being asked to believe not only that demand for AI will stay intense, but also that today's leaders can defend their place while prices fall and competition hardens. Alphabet sits quietly in the background as a reminder that existing profits can fund AI ambition without the same dramatic appeal to outside investors. In that sense, the IPO race looks less like a victory lap than a public search for time, money, and strategic breathing room in an industry that keeps getting more expensive.

sentences.original: The race to dominate artificial intelligence has become so expensive that the next major battle may be fought in public markets as much as in laboratories.
sentences.translation: 인공지능을 지배하려는 경쟁은 너무 비싸져서, 다음 주요 전투는 연구실 못지않게 주식시장에서도 치러질 수 있게 되었다.

grammarElements.text: The race to dominate artificial intelligence
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: that the next major battle may be fought in public markets as much as in laboratories
grammarElements.label: so ... that 결과절

vocabulary.example: A few firms hope to dominate the market before prices fall.

expressions.usage: 두 장소나 요소의 중요도를 비교할 때 쓰는 표현

patterns.explanation: 상황이 어느 정도까지 커져 결과가 바뀌었는지 설명하는 패턴
examples: The project became so costly that the team changed its plan.
The market became so crowded that smaller firms disappeared.

drillQuestions.question: The race ... has become so expensive that the next major battle may be ______ in public markets.

sentences.notes: so ... that 구조로 기술 경쟁의 무게중심이 연구실에서 자본시장으로 이동하고 있음을 한 번에 압축한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The race to dominate artificial intelligence

sentences.original: OpenAI, Anthropic, and the xAI-SpaceX combination all need staggering amounts of capital to build data centers, train models, and serve growing demand.
sentences.translation: 오픈AI, 앤스로픽, 그리고 xAI-스페이스X 결합체는 모두 데이터센터를 짓고 모델을 훈련시키며 늘어나는 수요를 감당하기 위해 엄청난 규모의 자본이 필요하다.

grammarElements.text: OpenAI, Anthropic, and the xAI-SpaceX combination
grammarElements.label: 병렬 주어

grammarElements.text: to build data centers, train models, and serve growing demand
grammarElements.label: 목적을 나타내는 to부정사

vocabulary.example: The plan would require staggering investment over the next decade.

expressions.usage: 공급과 인프라가 커지는 수요를 감당해야 할 때 자주 쓰는 표현

patterns.explanation: 여러 주체가 공통으로 필요로 하는 자원을 구체적인 목적과 함께 보여 주는 패턴
examples: Schools, clinics, and labs all need funding to hire staff, buy equipment, and expand services.
Startups all need time to test products, find users, and fix bugs.

drillQuestions.question: They all need staggering amounts of capital to build data centers, train models, and ______ growing demand.

sentences.notes: 세 회사를 한 문장에 병렬로 묶어 공통된 자본 압박이 얼마나 큰지 보여 준다.

sentences.original: That financing pressure has turned three bitter rivals into potential mega-IPO candidates at almost the same moment.
sentences.translation: 그 자금 조달 압박은 세 명의 앙숙 경쟁자를 거의 동시에 초대형 IPO 후보로 바꾸어 놓았다.

vocabulary.example: Former partners can become fierce rivals when money is scarce.

expressions.usage: 상황 변화가 주체의 성격이나 위치를 바꿨음을 말할 때 유용하다

patterns.explanation: 외부 압력이 사람이나 기업의 위치를 바꿨다고 설명하는 패턴
examples: The crisis has turned cautious firms into aggressive buyers.
Rising rates have turned homeowners into reluctant sellers.

drillQuestions.question: That financing pressure has turned three bitter rivals into potential mega-IPO ______.

sentences.notes: turn A into B 구조가 경쟁의 성격이 기술전에서 자본시장 게임으로 바뀌었음을 또렷하게 보여 준다.

sentences.original: The contest is not only financial, because each company is also selling a different story about what leadership in AI should look like.
sentences.translation: 이 경쟁은 단지 재무적인 것만이 아니다. 각 회사가 AI 리더십이 어떤 모습이어야 하는지에 대한 서로 다른 이야기를 함께 팔고 있기 때문이다.

grammarElements.text: because each company is also selling a different story
grammarElements.label: 이유절

grammarElements.text: about what leadership in AI should look like
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 안의 간접의문절

vocabulary.example: The contest for talent may be as important as the contest for customers.

expressions.usage: 문제의 차원이 여러 층위에 걸쳐 있음을 밝힐 때 쓰는 표현

patterns.explanation: 표면적 설명을 넘어서 더 넓은 층위를 보여 주는 패턴
examples: The issue is not only legal, because it is also cultural.
The slowdown is not only cyclical, because policy also matters.

drillQuestions.question: The contest is not only financial, because each company is also ______ a different story.

sentences.notes: not only와 because를 함께 써서 경쟁의 층위를 자금조달에서 서사 경쟁으로 확장한다.

sentences.original: OpenAI presents itself as the broad commercial platform, Anthropic leans on safety and enterprise discipline, and Elon Musk frames xAI as part of a far larger mission.
sentences.translation: 오픈AI는 자신을 폭넓은 상업 플랫폼으로 내세우고, 앤스로픽은 안전과 기업용 규율에 기대며, 일론 머스크는 xAI를 훨씬 더 큰 임무의 일부로 규정한다.

grammarElements.text: itself as the broad commercial platform
grammarElements.label: present A as B

grammarElements.text: xAI as part of a far larger mission
grammarElements.label: frame A as B

vocabulary.example: Investors often reward discipline when a market is overheated.

expressions.usage: 개념이나 회사를 특정 이미지로 설명할 때 많이 쓰인다

patterns.explanation: 여러 주체를 한 문장 안에서 비교하여 서로 다른 전략을 보여 주는 패턴
examples: One bank cut rates, another raised fees, and a third expanded lending.
One team chased growth, another protected margins, and a third restructured debt.

drillQuestions.question: Elon Musk frames xAI as part of a far larger ______.

sentences.notes: 세 주체를 병렬로 배치해 기술이 아니라 서사의 차별화를 보여 준다.

sentences.original: Their bosses also bring personal rivalry into every negotiation, which makes contracts, public statements, and fundraising rounds feel like moves in a long political game.
sentences.translation: 그들의 수장들은 모든 협상에 개인적 라이벌 의식도 끌고 들어오며, 그 탓에 계약과 공개 발언, 자금 조달 라운드가 긴 정치 게임의 수처럼 느껴지게 만든다.

grammarElements.text: which makes contracts, public statements, and fundraising rounds feel like moves in a long political game
grammarElements.label: 결과 관계절

vocabulary.example: Fundraising became harder after the market cooled.

expressions.usage: 행동이 자발적이라기보다 전략적 계산의 일부처럼 보일 때 쓴다

patterns.explanation: 원인 뒤에 which절을 붙여 인상이나 분위기를 설명하는 패턴
examples: The secrecy deepened, which made every update feel like a clue.
The pressure grew, which made routine meetings feel like tests.

drillQuestions.question: Their bosses also bring personal rivalry into every negotiation, which makes fundraising ______ feel like moves in a long political game.

sentences.notes: which절과 metaphor를 결합해 기업 행동이 전략 게임처럼 보이는 분위기를 만든다.

sentences.original: When companies need cash, status, and strategic advantage at the same time, even ordinary business decisions start to look theatrical.
sentences.translation: 기업들이 현금과 지위, 전략적 우위를 동시에 필요로 할 때, 평범한 사업 결정조차 연극적으로 보이기 시작한다.

grammarElements.text: When companies need cash, status, and strategic advantage at the same time
grammarElements.label: 시간/조건절

vocabulary.example: The rollout felt more theatrical than necessary.

expressions.usage: 여러 압력이 겹쳐 작동함을 보여 줄 때 기본적으로 쓰는 표현

patterns.pattern: When [multiple pressures], even [ordinary thing] starts to [unexpected result]
patterns.explanation: 정상적 행위가 비정상적으로 보이기 시작하는 조건을 설명하는 패턴
examples: When deadlines and fear rise together, even small questions start to feel dangerous.
When money and politics mix, even technical meetings start to look symbolic.

drillQuestions.question: Even ordinary business decisions start to look ______.

sentences.notes: when절 하나로 복수의 압박을 묶고, even을 통해 평범한 결정마저 달라 보이는 상황을 강조한다.

sentences.original: Those tensions are sharpest where business opportunity overlaps with government power.
sentences.translation: 그 긴장은 사업 기회와 정부 권력이 겹치는 지점에서 가장 날카로워진다.

grammarElements.text: where business opportunity overlaps with government power
grammarElements.label: 장소를 나타내는 관계부사절

vocabulary.example: Risk rises when commercial goals overlap with state policy.

expressions.usage: 정책, 계약, 규제 권한 등 국가의 영향력을 묶어 말할 때 쓴다

patterns.pattern: [Tension/problem] is sharpest where [force A] overlaps with [force B]
patterns.explanation: 두 힘이 만나는 지점에서 갈등이 가장 커진다고 설명하는 패턴
examples: Conflict is sharpest where identity overlaps with law.
Competition is sharpest where technology overlaps with national security.

drillQuestions.question: Those tensions are sharpest where business opportunity ______ with government power.

sentences.notes: 짧지만 where절이 핵심이다. 갈등이 어디서 가장 첨예해지는지 공간적으로 보여 준다.

sentences.original: Recent fights over defense work and regulatory positioning show that technical competition is now entangled with lobbying, reputation, and access to the state.
sentences.translation: 최근의 국방 계약과 규제 포지셔닝을 둘러싼 싸움은 기술 경쟁이 이제 로비, 평판, 그리고 국가 권력 접근성과 얽혀 있음을 보여 준다.

grammarElements.text: Recent fights over defense work and regulatory positioning
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: that technical competition is now entangled with lobbying, reputation, and access to the state
grammarElements.label: that절

grammarElements.text: with lobbying, reputation, and access to the state
grammarElements.label: 전치사구

vocabulary.example: Business goals became entangled with political goals.

expressions.usage: 정부 계약이나 정책 영향력에 닿을 수 있는 위치를 뜻할 때 쓰는 표현

patterns.pattern: [Recent events] show that [core activity] is now entangled with [other forces]
patterns.explanation: 최근 사례를 근거로 구조 변화나 복합성을 설명하는 패턴
examples: Recent rulings show that housing policy is now entangled with climate risk.
Recent lawsuits show that platform growth is entangled with labor law.

drillQuestions.question: Technical competition is now entangled with lobbying, reputation, and access to the ______.

sentences.notes: show that 구조를 써서 개별 분쟁을 더 큰 구조 변화의 증거로 해석한다.

sentences.original: At the same time, none of the firms is close to comfortable self-financing, because revenue is rising quickly while infrastructure spending is rising even faster.
sentences.translation: 동시에 어느 기업도 여유 있게 자체 자금 조달을 할 만큼 가까이 와 있지 않다. 매출은 빠르게 오르고 있지만 인프라 지출은 그보다 더 빠르게 증가하고 있기 때문이다.

grammarElements.text: because revenue is rising quickly while infrastructure spending is rising even faster
grammarElements.label: 이유절

vocabulary.example: True self-financing remains rare in capital-intensive sectors.

expressions.usage: 앞 문장과 다른 압박 요인을 덧붙일 때 연결어처럼 자주 쓴다

patterns.pattern: None of [group] is close to [goal], because [A] while [B]
patterns.explanation: 목표까지 아직 멀다는 판단과 그 이유를 비교 구조로 설명하는 패턴
examples: None of the teams is close to stability, because sales are rising while costs rise faster.
None of the projects is close to launch, because testing is advancing while bugs keep spreading.

drillQuestions.question: None of the firms is close to comfortable ______-financing.

sentences.notes: while 비교가 핵심이다. 매출 증가보다 지출 증가가 더 빠르다는 점이 자금 압박을 만든다.

sentences.original: That imbalance helps explain why public listing looks attractive even at enormous valuations and uncertain profit timelines.
sentences.translation: 그 불균형은 왜 공개 상장이 엄청난 밸류에이션과 불확실한 수익 일정 속에서도 매력적으로 보이는지를 설명해 준다.

grammarElements.text: even at enormous valuations and uncertain profit timelines
grammarElements.label: 양보 전치사구

vocabulary.example: A high valuation can attract attention even before profits arrive.

expressions.usage: 기업이 주식시장에 주식을 내놓는 행위를 가리키는 기본 표현

patterns.explanation: 앞 문장의 원인을 받아 특정 판단이나 현상의 이유를 설명할 때 쓰는 패턴
examples: That slowdown helps explain why hiring looks cautious.
That gap helps explain why reform looks urgent.

drillQuestions.question: That imbalance helps explain why public listing looks attractive even at enormous ______.

sentences.notes: 앞 문장과 논리적으로 연결되는 bridge sentence로, 상장이 왜 유혹적인 선택인지 해석해 준다.

sentences.original: If several giant offerings arrive together, markets may have to absorb a fundraising wave that would normally be spread across many years.
sentences.translation: 만약 여러 거대 공모가 한꺼번에 나온다면, 시장은 원래 여러 해에 걸쳐 분산되었을 자금 조달의 파도를 흡수해야 할지도 모른다.

grammarElements.text: If several giant offerings arrive together
grammarElements.label: 조건절

grammarElements.text: a fundraising wave that would normally be spread across many years
grammarElements.label: 목적어 + 관계절

vocabulary.example: Markets cannot always absorb new supply without strain.

expressions.usage: 시간적으로 나뉘어야 할 부담이나 흐름을 표현할 때 쓴다

patterns.explanation: 미래의 가능성과 그에 따른 부담을 말할 때 쓰는 전형적인 조건문 패턴
examples: If rates stay high, companies may have to cut spending.
If demand falls, producers may have to lower prices.

drillQuestions.question: Markets may have to ______ a fundraising wave.

sentences.notes: 조건절이 붙으면서 앞으로 시장이 감당해야 할 부담을 예측하는 문장이 된다.

sentences.original: Investors are being asked to believe not only that demand for AI will stay intense, but also that today's leaders can defend their place while prices fall and competition hardens.
sentences.translation: 투자자들은 AI 수요가 계속 뜨거울 것이라는 점뿐 아니라, 가격이 떨어지고 경쟁이 거세지는 동안에도 오늘의 선두주자들이 자신의 자리를 지킬 수 있다는 점까지 믿어 달라는 요구를 받고 있다.

grammarElements.text: not only that demand for AI will stay intense, but also that today's leaders can defend their place
grammarElements.label: 병렬 that절

vocabulary.example: Demand remained intense even after the initial excitement faded.

expressions.usage: 투자자가 동시에 믿어야 하는 두 조건을 병렬로 제시할 때 유용하다

patterns.pattern: [Subject] is being asked to believe not only [claim A], but also [claim B]
patterns.explanation: 상대가 감수해야 하는 복합적 믿음이나 전제를 정리할 때 쓰는 패턴
examples: Voters are being asked to believe not only the promise, but also the timeline.
Customers are being asked to believe not only the brand, but also the explanation.

drillQuestions.question: Investors are being asked to believe not only that demand for AI will stay ______.

sentences.notes: 수동태와 not only ... but also ...가 결합되어 투자자에게 요구되는 믿음의 수준을 강조한다.

sentences.original: Alphabet sits quietly in the background as a reminder that existing profits can fund AI ambition without the same dramatic appeal to outside investors.
sentences.translation: 알파벳은 기존 이익만으로도 외부 투자자에게 같은 수준의 극적인 호소를 하지 않고 AI 야망을 자금 조달할 수 있음을 상기시키는 존재로, 조용히 배경에 자리하고 있다.

grammarElements.text: that existing profits can fund AI ambition without the same dramatic appeal to outside investors
grammarElements.label: reminder의 내용절

vocabulary.example: The company avoided a public appeal because cash flow was strong.

expressions.usage: 중심 무대는 아니지만 중요한 비교 대상이 될 때 쓴다

patterns.pattern: [Company] sits in the background as a reminder that [fact]
patterns.explanation: 직접적으로 경쟁하지 않아도 비교 기준이 되는 존재를 설명하는 패턴
examples: Cash-rich incumbents sit in the background as a reminder that patience matters.
Older rivals sit in the background as a reminder that scale still counts.

drillQuestions.question: Alphabet sits quietly in the background as a reminder that existing profits can fund AI ______.

sentences.notes: background라는 표현으로 직접 서사의 중심은 아니지만 비교 기준이 되는 회사를 배치한다.

sentences.original: In that sense, the IPO race looks less like a victory lap than a public search for time, money, and strategic breathing room in an industry that keeps getting more expensive.
sentences.translation: 그런 의미에서 이 IPO 경쟁은 승리의 세리머니라기보다, 점점 더 비싸지는 산업 속에서 시간과 돈, 그리고 전략적 숨 돌릴 공간을 공개적으로 찾는 행위처럼 보인다.

grammarElements.text: a public search for time, money, and strategic breathing room
grammarElements.label: 보어

vocabulary.example: Extra funding gave the company breathing room for another year.

expressions.usage: 겉으로 보이는 해석을 부정하고 더 정확한 해석을 제시할 때 유용하다

patterns.pattern: [Thing] looks less like [surface reading] than [deeper reading]
patterns.explanation: 하나의 현상을 다시 해석하며 결론을 제시하는 비교 패턴
examples: The merger looks less like expansion than defense.
The speech looks less like celebration than warning.

drillQuestions.question: The IPO race looks less like a victory lap than a public search for time, money, and strategic ______ room.

sentences.notes: less like A than B 구조가 글 전체의 해석을 마무리하는 결론 문장 역할을 한다.
